bash_commands.command: grep -A 1 "@Deprecated" /workspace/kafka--0753c489/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java | grep -E "public static final|void |}" | grep -v "^--$" | head -50

awk '/@Deprecated/{deprecated=1; line=$0} deprecated && /public static final|public static|void /{print NR": "line; print NR": "$0; deprecated=0}' /workspace/kafka--0753c489/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java | head -80

awk '/\/\*\*.*@deprecated|@Deprecated/{flag=1} flag{print NR": "$0} /@Deprecated/{flag=0; getline; if(/@SuppressWarnings|public static/){print NR": "$0; flag=0}}' /workspace/kafka--0753c489/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java | head -120

  "text": "Apache Kafka 4.x Deprecated API Migration Inventory\n\nThis inventory catalogs all Java source files in the Apache Kafka repository (apache/kafka) that contain @Deprecated annotations on producer/streams configuration constants and exception-handling APIs.\n\n## Key Findings\n\n### Configuration Constants - StreamsConfig.java (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java)\nThe StreamsConfig class contains 21 deprecated configuration constants and documentation strings:\n\n1. **CACHE_MAX_BYTES_BUFFERING_CONFIG** (line 504): Replaced by STATESTORE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG since 3.4. This config was renamed to better reflect its purpose of caching state store data.\n\n2. **DEFAULT_DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG** (line 539): Replaced by DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG since 4.0. The \"DEFAULT_\" prefix was removed as part of API cleanup.\n\n3. **DEFAULT_PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG** (line 554): Replaced by PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG since 4.0. Same prefix removal as deserialization handler.\n\n4. **DEFAULT_DSL_STORE_CONFIG** (line 567): Replaced by DSL_STORE_SUPPLIERS_CLASS_CONFIG since 3.7. This reflects a major refactoring of the DSL store configuration to use a supplier pattern.\n\n5. **ROCKS_DB, IN_MEMORY, DEFAULT_DSL_STORE** (lines 572, 574, 576): Deprecated as part of the DSL store supplier refactoring. Now use BuiltInDslStoreSuppliers classes directly.\n\n6. **WINDOWED_INNER_CLASS_SERDE** (line 857): Replaced by class-specific constants in TimeWindowedSerializer, TimeWindowedDeserializer, SessionWindowedSerializer, and SessionWindowedDeserializer since 4.1.0.\n\n7. **WINDOW_SIZE_MS_CONFIG** (line 869): Replaced by TimeWindowedDeserializer#WINDOW_SIZE_MS_CONFIG since 4.1.0.\n\nAdditionally, the following DOC constants are deprecated with no explicit documented replacement:\n- BUFFERED_RECORDS_PER_PARTITION_DOC (line 494)\n- DEFAULT_CLIENT_SUPPLIER_DOC (line 531)\n- DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP_EXTRACTOR_CLASS_DOC (line 597)\n- ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_DOC (line 603)\n- MAX_TASK_IDLE_MS_DOC (line 631)\n- PROCESSING_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC (line 698)\n- RACK_AWARE_ASSIGNMENT_NON_OVERLAP_COST_DOC (line 730)\n- RACK_AWARE_ASSIGNMENT_STRATEGY_DOC (line 738)\n- RACK_AWARE_ASSIGNMENT_TRAFFIC_COST_DOC (line 753)\n- STATESTORE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES_DOC (line 806)\n- TASK_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC (line 817)\n\nAlso deprecated:\n- DUMMY_THREAD_INDEX (line 172): A public static final int with no documented replacement or purpose.\n\n### Configuration Constants - TopicConfig.java (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/TopicConfig.java)\nTwo deprecated configuration constants were found:\n\n1. **MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG** (line 227): Deprecated with no replacement because down-conversion is not possible in Apache Kafka 4.0 and newer. This configuration will be removed in Kafka 5.0.\n\n2. **MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_DOC** (line 233): Corresponding documentation string, also deprecated.\n\n### Exception Handler Method Signatures\n\n#### ProductionExceptionHandler Interface (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/ProductionExceptionHandler.java)\nTwo method signatures are deprecated, both in favor of new versions that include ErrorHandlerContext:\n\n1. **handle(ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** (line 41): Deprecated since 3.9 in favor of handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception). The new signature provides access to error context metadata.\n\n2. **handleSerializationException(ProducerRecord, Exception)** (line 80): Deprecated since 3.9 in favor of handleSerializationException(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord, Exception, SerializationExceptionOrigin). The new signature includes context metadata and the origin of the serialization exception.\n\n#### DeserializationExceptionHandler Interface (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/DeserializationExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProcessorContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** (line 49): Deprecated since 3.9 in favor of handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception). The new version uses ErrorHandlerContext instead of ProcessorContext for better abstraction.\n\n#### DefaultProductionExceptionHandler Implementation (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/DefaultProductionExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** override (line 35): Deprecated since 3.9, implements the deprecated interface method. Replaced by the new handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord, Exception) override on the same class.\n\n#### LogAndFailExceptionHandler Implementation (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/LogAndFailExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProcessorContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** override (line 40): Deprecated since 3.9. The new implementation uses handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ConsumerRecord, Exception).\n\n#### LogAndContinueExceptionHandler Implementation (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/LogAndContinueExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProcessorContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** override (line 40): Deprecated since 3.9. The new implementation uses handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ConsumerRecord, Exception).\n\n### StreamsConfig Methods (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java)\n\nTwo convenience methods are deprecated since 4.0:\n\n1. **defaultDeserializationExceptionHandler()** (line 2115): Replaced by deserializationExceptionHandler() which internally checks both the new and deprecated config names with appropriate fallback logic.\n\n2. **defaultProductionExceptionHandler()** (line 2137): Replaced by productionExceptionHandler() which implements similar fallback logic.\n\n## Migration Strategy Summary\n\nThe primary deprecation patterns in Kafka 4.x include:\n\n1. **Config Prefix Removal**: DEFAULT_* prefixes were removed from exception handler config names for consistency (3 symbols).\n\n2. **DSL Store Refactoring**: Major refactoring from simple string constants (ROCKS_DB, IN_MEMORY) to a supplier-based architecture (DSL_STORE_SUPPLIERS_CLASS_CONFIG) (4 symbols).\n\n3. **Exception Handler API Evolution**: Shift from ProcessorContext to ErrorHandlerContext for better abstraction and access to error metadata (6 method signatures).\n\n4. **Serialization Config Consolidation**: Window serialization config consolidated into specific serializer/deserializer classes (2 symbols).\n\n5. **Feature Removal**: Down-conversion feature removed entirely (2 symbols in TopicConfig).\n\n6. **Documentation Constants**: Many DOC constants deprecated without explicit replacements, likely as part of code cleanup (11 symbols).\n\nTotal deprecated symbols cataloged: 31 configuration constants/strings and 8 method signatures across 7 files."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Kafka @Deprecated API Migration Inventory

## Your Task

Catalog all Java source files in `apache/kafka` that contain `@Deprecated` annotations on producer/streams configuration constants or exception-handling APIs. Focus on:

1. **StreamsConfig.java** — find deprecated config constants (e.g., `CACHE_MAX_BYTES_BUFFERING_CONFIG`, `DEFAULT_DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG`, `DEFAULT_PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG`, `DEFAULT_DSL_STORE_CONFIG`) and note their replacements.
2. **Exception handler interfaces and implementations** — find deprecated `handle()` method signatures in `ProductionExceptionHandler`, `DefaultProductionExceptionHandler`, `DeserializationExceptionHandler`, `LogAndFailExceptionHandler`, and `LogAndContinueExceptionHandler`.
3. **TopicConfig.java** — find deprecated config constants (e.g., `MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG`).

For each file, report the repo, file path, deprecated symbol name, and what it was replaced by (if documented in the annotation or Javadoc).

## Context

You are working on a migration inventory task for the Apache Kafka ecosystem. The pinned Kafka snapshot is version 4.x — note that older deprecated producer configs like `block.on.buffer.full` and `metadata.fetch.timeout.ms` were fully removed prior to this version and will not appear.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` (apache/kafka)
- `sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` (apache/flink)
- `sg-evals/camel--1006f047` (apache/camel)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.java"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.java", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.java", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you catalog all Java source files in Apache Kafka that contain `@Deprecated` annotations on producer/streams configuration constants and exception-handling APIs. Let me start by exploring the repository structure.
Now let me search for all `@Deprecated` annotations in the kafka repo to get a comprehensive view:
Let me use a more efficient approach to extract all deprecated symbols in StreamsConfig.java:
Now let me examine the exception handler files:
Now let me get more context from StreamsConfig.java to identify replacements for the deprecated config constants. Let me search for JavaDoc comments that explain the deprecations:
Now let me create a comprehensive inventory by searching for any additional deprecated symbols in the kafka repository that I might have missed:
Now let me create the comprehensive answer.json file with all my findings:

Perfect! I have successfully created a comprehensive inventory of all Java source files in Apache Kafka that contain `@Deprecated` annotations on producer/streams configuration constants and exception-handling APIs. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings

### **7 Java files cataloged** across 2 main areas:

#### **Configuration Constants (StreamsConfig.java & TopicConfig.java)**
- **23 deprecated configuration constants**, including:
  - `CACHE_MAX_BYTES_BUFFERING_CONFIG` → replaced by `STATESTORE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG`
  - `DEFAULT_DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG` → replaced by `DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG`
  - `DEFAULT_PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG` → replaced by `PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG`
  - `DEFAULT_DSL_STORE_CONFIG` → replaced by `DSL_STORE_SUPPLIERS_CLASS_CONFIG`
  - `WINDOWED_INNER_CLASS_SERDE` → replaced by class-specific constants in TimeWindowedSerializer, TimeWindowedDeserializer, etc.
  - `MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG` (TopicConfig) - no replacement, feature removed in Kafka 4.0+

#### **Exception Handler Method Signatures**
- **8 deprecated method signatures** in exception handlers:
  - `ProductionExceptionHandler.handle(ProducerRecord, Exception)` → `handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord, Exception)`
  - `DeserializationExceptionHandler.handle(ProcessorContext, ...)` → `handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ...)`
  - Implementations in: `DefaultProductionExceptionHandler`, `LogAndFailExceptionHandler`, `LogAndContinueExceptionHandler`

#### **StreamsConfig Methods**
- `defaultDeserializationExceptionHandler()` → `deserializationExceptionHandler()`
- `defaultProductionExceptionHandler()` → `productionExceptionHandler()`

The complete inventory with file paths, line numbers, and replacement details has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`.

arguments.description: Extract deprecated symbol declarations in StreamsConfig.java
Extract line numbers and declarations of @Deprecated symbols

arguments.command: awk '/@Deprecated/{deprecated=1; line=$0} deprecated && /public static final|public static|void /{print NR": "line; print NR": "$0; deprecated=0}' /workspace/kafka--0753c489/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java | head -80

awk '/\/\*\*.*@deprecated|@Deprecated/{flag=1} flag{print NR": "$0} /@Deprecated/{flag=0; getline; if(/@SuppressWarnings|public static/){print NR": "$0; flag=0}}' /workspace/kafka--0753c489/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java | head -120

arguments.description: Find all @deprecated JavaDoc tags in StreamsConfig
Find all files with @Deprecated in streams source

  "text": "Apache Kafka 4.x Deprecated API Migration Inventory\n\nThis inventory catalogs all Java source files in the Apache Kafka repository (apache/kafka) that contain @Deprecated annotations on producer/streams configuration constants and exception-handling APIs.\n\n## Key Findings\n\n### Configuration Constants - StreamsConfig.java (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java)\nThe StreamsConfig class contains 21 deprecated configuration constants and documentation strings:\n\n1. **CACHE_MAX_BYTES_BUFFERING_CONFIG** (line 504): Replaced by STATESTORE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES_CONFIG since 3.4. This config was renamed to better reflect its purpose of caching state store data.\n\n2. **DEFAULT_DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG** (line 539): Replaced by DESERIALIZATION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG since 4.0. The \"DEFAULT_\" prefix was removed as part of API cleanup.\n\n3. **DEFAULT_PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG** (line 554): Replaced by PRODUCTION_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_CONFIG since 4.0. Same prefix removal as deserialization handler.\n\n4. **DEFAULT_DSL_STORE_CONFIG** (line 567): Replaced by DSL_STORE_SUPPLIERS_CLASS_CONFIG since 3.7. This reflects a major refactoring of the DSL store configuration to use a supplier pattern.\n\n5. **ROCKS_DB, IN_MEMORY, DEFAULT_DSL_STORE** (lines 572, 574, 576): Deprecated as part of the DSL store supplier refactoring. Now use BuiltInDslStoreSuppliers classes directly.\n\n6. **WINDOWED_INNER_CLASS_SERDE** (line 857): Replaced by class-specific constants in TimeWindowedSerializer, TimeWindowedDeserializer, SessionWindowedSerializer, and SessionWindowedDeserializer since 4.1.0.\n\n7. **WINDOW_SIZE_MS_CONFIG** (line 869): Replaced by TimeWindowedDeserializer#WINDOW_SIZE_MS_CONFIG since 4.1.0.\n\nAdditionally, the following DOC constants are deprecated with no explicit documented replacement:\n- BUFFERED_RECORDS_PER_PARTITION_DOC (line 494)\n- DEFAULT_CLIENT_SUPPLIER_DOC (line 531)\n- DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP_EXTRACTOR_CLASS_DOC (line 597)\n- ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_DOC (line 603)\n- MAX_TASK_IDLE_MS_DOC (line 631)\n- PROCESSING_EXCEPTION_HANDLER_CLASS_DOC (line 698)\n- RACK_AWARE_ASSIGNMENT_NON_OVERLAP_COST_DOC (line 730)\n- RACK_AWARE_ASSIGNMENT_STRATEGY_DOC (line 738)\n- RACK_AWARE_ASSIGNMENT_TRAFFIC_COST_DOC (line 753)\n- STATESTORE_CACHE_MAX_BYTES_DOC (line 806)\n- TASK_TIMEOUT_MS_DOC (line 817)\n\nAlso deprecated:\n- DUMMY_THREAD_INDEX (line 172): A public static final int with no documented replacement or purpose.\n\n### Configuration Constants - TopicConfig.java (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/TopicConfig.java)\nTwo deprecated configuration constants were found:\n\n1. **MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_CONFIG** (line 227): Deprecated with no replacement because down-conversion is not possible in Apache Kafka 4.0 and newer. This configuration will be removed in Kafka 5.0.\n\n2. **MESSAGE_DOWNCONVERSION_ENABLE_DOC** (line 233): Corresponding documentation string, also deprecated.\n\n### Exception Handler Method Signatures\n\n#### ProductionExceptionHandler Interface (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/ProductionExceptionHandler.java)\nTwo method signatures are deprecated, both in favor of new versions that include ErrorHandlerContext:\n\n1. **handle(ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** (line 41): Deprecated since 3.9 in favor of handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception). The new signature provides access to error context metadata.\n\n2. **handleSerializationException(ProducerRecord, Exception)** (line 80): Deprecated since 3.9 in favor of handleSerializationException(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord, Exception, SerializationExceptionOrigin). The new signature includes context metadata and the origin of the serialization exception.\n\n#### DeserializationExceptionHandler Interface (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/DeserializationExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProcessorContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** (line 49): Deprecated since 3.9 in favor of handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception). The new version uses ErrorHandlerContext instead of ProcessorContext for better abstraction.\n\n#### DefaultProductionExceptionHandler Implementation (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/DefaultProductionExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProducerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** override (line 35): Deprecated since 3.9, implements the deprecated interface method. Replaced by the new handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ProducerRecord, Exception) override on the same class.\n\n#### LogAndFailExceptionHandler Implementation (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/LogAndFailExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProcessorContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** override (line 40): Deprecated since 3.9. The new implementation uses handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ConsumerRecord, Exception).\n\n#### LogAndContinueExceptionHandler Implementation (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/errors/LogAndContinueExceptionHandler.java)\n\n**handle(ProcessorContext, ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>, Exception)** override (line 40): Deprecated since 3.9. The new implementation uses handle(ErrorHandlerContext, ConsumerRecord, Exception).\n\n### StreamsConfig Methods (streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/StreamsConfig.java)\n\nTwo convenience methods are deprecated since 4.0:\n\n1. **defaultDeserializationExceptionHandler()** (line 2115): Replaced by deserializationExceptionHandler() which internally checks both the new and deprecated config names with appropriate fallback logic.\n\n2. **defaultProductionExceptionHandler()** (line 2137): Replaced by productionExceptionHandler() which implements similar fallback logic.\n\n## Migration Strategy Summary\n\nThe primary deprecation patterns in Kafka 4.x include:\n\n1. **Config Prefix Removal**: DEFAULT_* prefixes were removed from exception handler config names for consistency (3 symbols).\n\n2. **DSL Store Refactoring**: Major refactoring from simple string constants (ROCKS_DB, IN_MEMORY) to a supplier-based architecture (DSL_STORE_SUPPLIERS_CLASS_CONFIG) (4 symbols).\n\n3. **Exception Handler API Evolution**: Shift from ProcessorContext to ErrorHandlerContext for better abstraction and access to error metadata (6 method signatures).\n\n4. **Serialization Config Consolidation**: Window serialization config consolidated into specific serializer/deserializer classes (2 symbols).\n\n5. **Feature Removal**: Down-conversion feature removed entirely (2 symbols in TopicConfig).\n\n6. **Documentation Constants**: Many DOC constants deprecated without explicit replacements, likely as part of code cleanup (11 symbols).\n\nTotal deprecated symbols cataloged: 31 configuration constants/strings and 8 method signatures across 7 files."
}
